perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS 1.1 “Invention”, the Pipeline Piranha Invention, Patent Pending #103573330A, shall mean all information relating to products, applications, systems, components, technologies and business topics. Including but not limited to prototypes, drawings, data, trade secrets and in tellectual property relating to the “Patent Pending” invention named Pipeline Piranha.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS 1.1 “Invention”, shall mean any concept, idea, design, or creation disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, regardless of whether it is patented or patent pending. For clarity, the Pipeline Piranha Invention, Patent Pending #103573330A, shall specifically be excluded from this definition.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by redefining "Invention" to explicitly exclude the Pipeline Piranha Invention, which was previously the main subject of the agreement. This contradicts the Background section, which states the parties wish to discuss the "Invention" (referring to the Pipeline Piranha). The change creates uncertainty about the scope of confidential information covered by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BACKGROUND The Disclosing Party and Receiving Party wish to discuss and exchange certain items and information (the “Inven tion”) which the parties hereto consider highly confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes the 'Pipeline Piranha Invention' from the definition of 'Invention'. The contradicted text (BACKGROUND) states the parties wish to discuss the 'Invention', which clearly refers to the Pipeline Piranha based on the original definition. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed definition now excludes the very subject the parties intended to discuss and consider confidential, thus directly conflicting with the background section. This creates uncertainty about the scope of confidential information covered by the agreement, and therefore creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 60 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party is not required to return all information to the Disclosing Party and can retain the copies.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. The first sentence remains the same, establishing that the Disclosing Party retains ownership of the confidential information. However, the added text changes the return policy from 15 to 60 days and eliminates the requirement to return information after a decision is made. This directly conflicts with the third sentence, which requires returning all information without copies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 60 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party is not required to return all information to the Disclosing Party and can retain the copies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The explanation states that it alters the return policy from requiring all information to be returned without copies to allowing the receiving party to retain copies, which contradicts the initial sentence stating all information remains the property of the disclosing party. The changed text is the same as the contradicted text because it's an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. NO LICENSE Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. NO LICENSE Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right. However, the Receiving Party shall have a limited, non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal evaluation purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that no license is granted. The added sentence grants a limited license for internal evaluation. This is a direct contradiction regarding whether or not a license to use the confidential information is granted, leading to legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. NO LICENSE Neither party does, by virtue of disclosure of the Confidential Information, grant, either expressly or by implication, estopp els or otherwise, any right or license to any patent, trade secret, invention, trad emark, copyright, or other intellectual property right. However, the Receiving Party shall have a limited, non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal evaluation purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that no license is granted for the confidential information. The changed text adds a sentence that grants a limited, non-exclusive license for internal evaluation. This directly contradicts the initial statement that no license is granted, creating an in-text contradiction and fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Agreement shall be binding upon either party unless reduced in writing and signed by them.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. Verbal agreements amending this contract are acceptable and binding, overriding the need for written and signed amendments.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that any changes to the agreement must be in writing and signed. The addition contradicts this directly by stating that verbal agreements are acceptable and binding, thus introducing an in-text contradiction about how the contract can be modified and creating uncertainty in the enforcement of future modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement sets forth all of the co venants, promises, agreements, conditions and understandings between the parties an d there are no covenants, promises, agreements or conditions, either oral or written, between them other than herein set forth. Verbal agreements amending this contract are acceptable and binding, overriding the need for written and signed amendments.
perturbation.justification: The original clause (and the contradicted text which is the same) specifies that alterations to the agreement must be in writing and signed. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that verbal agreements are acceptable and binding, overriding the need for written and signed amendments. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', causing uncertainty about the enforcement of contract modifications.

perturbation.original_text: 2. USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) Receive and maintain the Confidential Information in confidence; (b) Examine the Confidential Information at its own expense; (c) Not reproduce the Confidential Information or any part thereof without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (d) Not, directly or indirectly , make known, divulge, publish or communicate the Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (e) Limit the internal dissemination of the Confidential Information and the internal disclosure of the Confid ential Information received from the Disclosing Party to those officers and employees, if any, of the Receiving Party who have a need to know and an obligation to protect it; (f) Not use or utilize the Confidential Information without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 3 (g) Not use the Confidential Information or any part thereof as a basis for the design or creation of any method, system, apparatus or device similar to any method, system, apparatus or device embodied in the Confidential Information unless expressly authorized in writing by Disclosing Party; a n d (h) Utilize the best efforts possible to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information from loss, theft, destruction, or the like.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) Receive and maintain the Confidential Information in confidence; (b) Examine the Confidential Information at its own expense; (c) Not reproduce the Confidential Information or any part thereof without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (d) Not, directly or indirectly , make known, divulge, publish or communicate the Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (e) Limit the internal dissemination of the Confidential Information and the internal disclosure of the Confid ential Information received from the Disclosing Party to those officers and employees, if any, of the Receiving Party who have a need to know and an obligation to protect it; (f) Not use or utilize the Confidential Information without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 3 (g) Not use the Confidential Information or any part thereof as a basis for the design or creation of any method, system, apparatus or device similar to any method, system, apparatus or device embodied in the Confidential Information unless expressly authorized in writing by Disclosing Party; a n d (h) While the Receiving Party should utilize best efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information, they will not be held liable for loss due to unforeseen circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in Section 2. While the original clause (h) states that the Receiving Party will use the best efforts possible to protect the Confidential Information, the addition to clause (h) removes liability for loss due to unforeseen circumstances. This contradicts the initial obligation to actively protect the information and introduces uncertainty regarding the Receiving Party's responsibility in case of a security breach or accidental disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) Receive and maintain the Confidential Information in confidence; (b) Examine the Confidential Information at its own expense; (c) Not reproduce the Confidential Information or any part thereof without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (d) Not, directly or indirectly , make known, divulge, publish or communicate the Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (e) Limit the internal dissemination of the Confidential Information and the internal disclosure of the Confid ential Information received from the Disclosing Party to those officers and employees, if any, of the Receiving Party who have a need to know and an obligation to protect it; (f) Not use or utilize the Confidential Information without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 3 (g) Not use the Confidential Information or any part thereof as a basis for the design or creation of any method, system, apparatus or device similar to any method, system, apparatus or device embodied in the Confidential Information unless expressly authorized in writing by Disclosing Party; a n d (h) While the Receiving Party should utilize best efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information, they will not be held liable for loss due to unforeseen circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause (h) of Section 2. Originally, the Receiving Party agreed to 'Utilize the best efforts possible to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information'. The changed text adds 'While the Receiving Party should utilize best efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information, they will not be held liable for loss due to unforeseen circumstances.' This directly contradicts the original obligation to actively protect the information by removing liability for loss due to unforeseen circumstances. This weakens the protection and creates a clear conflict regarding the Receiving Party's responsibility, which aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10. ARBITRATION Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement, or the breach thereof, shall be resolved by arbitration conducted by the Commercial Division of the American Arbitration Asso ciation and in accordance with the rules thereof, or in any other convenient forum agreed to in writing by the parties. Any arbitration award shall be final and binding, and judgment upon the award rendered pursuant to such arbitration may be entered in any court of proper jurisdiction. Notwithstanding the forego ing, either party may seek and obtain temporary injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction against any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. ARBITRATION Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement, or the breach thereof, shall be resolved by arbitration conducted by the Commercial Division of the American Arbitration Asso ciation and in accordance with the rules thereof, or in any other convenient forum agreed to in writing by the parties. Any arbitration award shall be non-binding, and either party may pursue litigation following the arbitration. Notwithstanding the forego ing, either party may seek and obtain temporary injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction against any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change makes the arbitration award non-binding, directly contradicting the original text's declaration that the arbitration award shall be final and binding. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the finality of arbitration and whether further legal action can be pursued, undermining the purpose of the arbitration clause. The statement, 'Any arbitration award shall be non-binding, and either party may pursue litigation following the arbitration' directly opposes the original statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. ARBITRATION Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this agreement, or the breach thereof, shall be resolved by arbitration conducted by the Commercial Division of the American Arbitration Asso ciation and in accordance with the rules thereof, or in any other convenient forum agreed to in writing by the parties. Any arbitration award shall be non-binding, and either party may pursue litigation following the arbitration. Notwithstanding the forego ing, either party may seek and obtain temporary injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction against any improper disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 10 (Arbitration) to state that 'Any arbitration award shall be non-binding, and either party may pursue litigation following the arbitration.' This directly contradicts the original text, which stated that 'Any arbitration award shall be final and binding'. The Contradicted Text in the prompt is a replication of the Changed Text, where the contradictory statement exists, thus confirming the presence of a strong in-text contradiction, because the Changed Text negates the finality of the arbitration award.
